title: Pathmind
description: A visual sandbox for designing automation workflows in Minecraft
body:
A visual sandbox for designing automation workflows in Minecraft

🇺🇸 English · 🇩🇪 Deutsch · 🇪🇸 Español · 🇫🇷 Français · 🇵🇱 Polski · 🇧🇷 Português (BR) · 🇷🇺 Русский

## Features ### Visual Automation - **Node-Based Editor**: Build automation visually with nodes, connections, and editable parameters instead of commands or scripts - **Readable Workflows**: Lay out your logic in a way that is easy to follow, revisit, and expand over time - **Creative Sandbox**: Experiment with behaviors, interactions, and systems inside a dedicated in-game workspace ### Workspace And Presets - **Saved Graphs**: Keep your automation setups as reusable presets you can come back to later - **Organized Editing**: Work across multiple presets and continue refining them as your ideas grow - **Import And Share**: Bring in community-made setups or use your own presets as building blocks for larger systems ### In-Game Execution - **Live Workflow Runtime**: Run graphs directly in Minecraft and watch them respond while you play - **Visual Feedback**: See execution state, runtime feedback, and validation cues while building and testing - **Flexible Logic**: Combine actions, conditions, reusable logic, and event-driven behavior into larger workflows ### Navigation And Interaction - **Movement Automation**: Build workflows around movement, navigation, and world interaction - **Expanded Integrations**: Optional integrations can extend what Pathmind can do in supported setups - **Built For Play**: Designed to feel like a Minecraft tool, not an external scripting environment ### Community Marketplace - **Browse Presets In-Game**: Discover automation setups made by other players without leaving Minecraft - **Publish Your Own Work**: Share your presets with the community through the built-in marketplace - **Start From Examples**: Import existing presets and adapt them to your own world, style, or ideas ## Video Showcase ## Quick Start ### Prerequisites - **Minecraft**: 1.21 through 1.21.11 - **Fabric Loader**: 0.17.3 or higher - **Fabric API**: version matching your Minecraft version - **Java**: 21 or higher ### Installation 1. **Install Fabric Loader** - Download and install Fabric Loader for your Minecraft version - [Download from FabricMC](https://fabricmc.net/use/installer/) 2. **Install Fabric API** - Download the matching Fabric API release for your Minecraft version - [Download from Modrinth](https://modrinth.com/mod/fabric-api) 3. **Install Pathmind** - Download the Pathmind jar for your Minecraft version - Place it in your `mods` folder 4. **Launch and Enjoy** - Start Minecraft with Fabric - Use your configured keybind to open the visual editor ### Opening the Editor - Use your configured keybind to open Pathmind - The visual editor opens in fullscreen mode ### Creating Workflows 1. **Add Nodes**: Drag nodes from the sidebar onto the workspace 2. **Connect Logic**: Link nodes together to shape how the workflow behaves 3. **Configure Parameters**: Edit node values to match the behavior you want 4. **Organize Your Graph**: Move nodes around to keep your setup readable 5. **Run And Refine**: Test your workflow in-game and keep improving it ## Disclaimer Pathmind is designed for automation and workflow visualization in **single-player** or in **multiplayer environments where such tools are explicitly permitted**. You are solely responsible for ensuring that your use of this mod does **not violate any server rules, terms of service, or community guidelines**. Do not use Pathmind in ways that provide unfair advantages, disrupt gameplay for others, or break the terms set by server administrators or Minecraft service providers.
